``` ::: ::: ::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} # Parole Eligibility ## How Parole Eligibility is Determined Most people in prison earn one day of credit for each day served. Credits are deducted from judicial maximum terms to produce earlier mandatory release dates. So-called “indeterminate” sentences carry minimum and maximum terms that are specified in statute and cannot be varied by the court when imposing a prison sentence. There is no standard ratio between minimum and maximum terms. The other sentence type “definite sentences” include terms to parole release eligibility that are set by statutory formula rather than the judicial sentence; the default is parole-release eligibility after 25 percent.
## In Prison Past Their Parole Eligibility Year In 2020, there were 1,322 people in prison past their parole eligibility year. This group made up 33% of people in prison for new crimes and with sentence lengths between 1 to 25 years. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Demographics The demographics of people in prison past their parole eligibility year reveal notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and black, non-hispanic people. Gender distribution indicates a predominance of males over females. Age-wise, the majority of people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Criminal Offenses ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
In 2020, 70% of people in prison past their parole eligibility year were in prison for non-violent offenses. The breakdown of criminal offenses of people in prison past their parole eligibility year reveals a varied landscape, with the majority of people incarcerated for property (37%) and public order (18%) offenses.
``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Original Sentence Lengths In 2020, among the prison population eligible for parole but not yet released, people with sentences between 10 to 24.9 years constituted the majority, representing 60%. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Missing Data
# Prison Population ## Prison Population Trends From 2010 to 2022, the prison population decreased 12%, changing from 6,681 in 2010 to 5,847 in 2022. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

# Prison Releases ## Prison Release Trends From 2010 to 2020, prison releases increased 13%, dropping from 2,970 in 2010 to 3,342 in 2020. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Conditional Releases Conditional release involves an individual’s release under specific conditions and supervision, whereas unconditional release means the individual is released without further obligations or restrictions. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::
## Demographics of People Released The demographics of people released from prison reveal notable proportions among White, non-Hispanic and black, non-hispanic people. Gender distribution indicates a predominance of males over females. Age-wise, the majority of people were 25-34 years and 35-44 years old. These findings provide insights into the populations transitioning back into the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: :::

``` ::: :::
# Disparities ## Incarceration Rates In West Virginia, Black, non-Hispanic people are incarcerated in state prison at a rate 3.9 times higher than White, non-Hispanic people, when accounting for population sizes in the community. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

## Years Spent in Prison After Parole Eligibility In 2020 , people in prison past their parole eligibility year collectively spent a total of 0 years behind bars instead of supervised in the community on parole. ## Timing of Release After Parole Eligibility The timing of release for individuals after their parole eligibility year varied by offense type and demographic group, with many remaining in prison for several years beyond their eligibility date. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
